zh.Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Eng: 英國格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 奧 澤福
zh.Restormel, Cornwall, England: 英國可瓦爾雷斯托梅爾
zh.Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛
zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda Fitz Roger於大約1155年出生在英國戴文武波利時，她的父親Simon FitzRichard43歲，母親Adeliza de Hearn69歲。她於1172年與Robert de Cardinham II在英國薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1234年在英國爾享年80歲。

zh-hans.Restormel, Cornwall, England: 英国可瓦爾雷斯托梅爾

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda Fitz Roger于大约1155年出生在英国戴文武波利时，她的父亲Simon FitzRichard43岁，母亲Adeliza de Hearn69岁。她于1172年与Robert de Cardinham II在英国薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1234年在英国爾享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Rogero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Cayetano, Jose, Silva.
Spanish: from the personal name Rogero (see Roger ).
Catalan (Rogeró): from a pet form of the personal name Roger .

variants.name: Rieck
meanings: South German: from a pet form of the personal name Ru(o)diger, a compound of Old High German hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + gēr ‘spear, lance’ (see Roger ).
North German: from a short form of a personal name containing the element rīc ‘power(ful)’.
Germanized form of Sorbian Rjek: nickname from Upper Sorbian rjek ‘hero’. Compare Reck .

variants.name: Rogelio
meanings: Spanish; Portuguese (Rogélio): from the personal name Rogelio, Portuguese Rogélio, of ancient Germanic origin (see Roger ).

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Roger  .

variants.name: Rogers
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Roger , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Ruaidhrí (see Rorie ).

History: Thomas Rogers (c. 1587–1621), born in London, England, was among the Pilgrim Fathers who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620. He died during the first winter at Plymouth Colony, but his son Joseph survived and married, and was later joined in MA by his brother John. This name was subsequently brought to North America independently by many different bearers.

variants.name: Rogel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Jose Luis, Juan, Marcos, Margarita, Wilfredo, Adilia, Alejandro, Ana Gladys, Ana Maria, Anacleto.
Spanish: from the personal name Rogel, an equivalent of French Roger .

Americanized form of Slovenian Rogelj: nickname or topographic name from rogelj ‘horn, point’, figuratively ‘tall young man’, as a topographic name probably denoting someone who lived e.g. on a horn-shaped spur of a hill. The surname in the form Rogel is found in Slovenia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Prosser
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Rhosier ‘son of Roger’ (see Roger and Rosser ).

variants.name: Rogerson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Roger + -son.

variants.name: Ritger
meanings: Altered form of German and Dutch Rutger or Rütger, from the personal name Rudiger (see Roger ).

variants.name: Rogge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Elke, Hans, Helmut, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Roger  .
North German and Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a grower of rye or a baker of rye bread, from Low German, Dutch rogge ‘rye’.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Fitz Roger was born about 1155, in Umberleigh, Devon, England, her father, Simon FitzRichard, was 43 and her mother, Adeliza de Hearn, was 69. She married Robert de Cardinham II in 1172, in Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1234, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda Fitz Roger est née environ 1155, à Umberleigh, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Simon FitzRichard, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Adeliza de Hearn, avait 69 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Cardinham II en 1172, à Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1234, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda Fitz Roger um 1155 in Umberleigh, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Simon FitzRichard 43 und ihre Mutter Adeliza de Hearn 69. Sie hat 1172 in Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England Robert de Cardinham II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1234 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda Fitz Roger è nata il circa 1155 nel Umberleigh, Devon, England, sua padre, Simon FitzRichard, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Adeliza de Hearn, aveva 69 anni. Lei sposato Robert de Cardinham II nel 1172, nel Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1234, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 80 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda Fitz Rogerが約1155年にイギリスデボンウンバーレイグで生まれた時，父Simon FitzRichardは43歳，母Adeliza de Hearnは69歳であった。1172年にイギリスソメルセトチルトン カンテロでRobert de Cardinham IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1234年にイギリスチェシュアイアで80歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Fitz Roger(이)가 대략 1155년 영국 데폰 벌리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Simon FitzRichard(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adeliza de Hearn(은)는 69세였다. 그녀는 1172년 영국 서머세트 치턴 캉로에서 Robert de Cardinham II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1234년 영국 체실르에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England: Chilton Cantelo, Bro an Hañv, Английн

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda Fitz Roger nasceu aproximadamente 1155, em Umberleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Simon FitzRichard, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Adeliza de Hearn, tinha 69 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert de Cardinham II em 1172, em Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1234, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 80 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda Fitz Roger родилась около 1155 г. в местности умбирлиг, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Simon FitzRichard было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Adeliza de Hearn, 69. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Cardinham II в 1172 г. в местности чилтон кантелоу, сомирсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1234 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 80 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda Fitz Roger nació aproximadamente 1155, en Umberleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Simon FitzRichard, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Adeliza de Hearn, tenía 69 años. Se casó con Robert de Cardinham II en 1172, en Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1234, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años.
